title: Define One Browser Boundary for CORS and Proxying
prompt: Use $nextjs-nestjs-integration to improve this code according to integration-cors-proxy-contract.
notes: Choose one browser boundary. If browsers call Next.js only, keep NestJS internal. If browsers call NestJS directly, configure explicit CORS and cookie policy.

title: Verify Split Deployment Health and Runtime Alignment
prompt: Use $nextjs-nestjs-integration to improve this code according to integration-deployment-split.
notes: Split deployments need health and contract gates. Verify the NestJS API is reachable and the Next.js generated client matches the current OpenAPI document.

title: Generate Clients from NestJS OpenAPI Contracts
prompt: Use $nextjs-nestjs-integration to improve this code according to integration-openapi-client-boundary.
notes: Use generated clients or shared contract packages for NestJS APIs. Handwritten fetch wrappers drift from DTOs, response envelopes, and error contracts.

title: Server Components Should Fetch Backend Data Directly
prompt: Use $nextjs-nestjs-integration to improve this code according to integration-server-component-fetching.
notes: Server Components should not call the Next.js app's own Route Handlers. Directly use the NestJS client or server-only DAL to avoid build-time failures and extra HTTP round trips.

title: Propagate Session Context Explicitly to NestJS
prompt: Use $nextjs-nestjs-integration to improve this code according to integration-session-propagation.
notes: Propagate auth intentionally. Do not forward arbitrary browser headers or trust client-provided identity headers.

title: Split Public, Server-only, and Backend Env Contracts
prompt: Use $nextjs-nestjs-integration to improve this code according to integration-shared-env-contract.
notes: In a monorepo, env files can look shared while runtime exposure differs. Keep browser-public, Next.js server-only, and NestJS backend secrets separate.
